zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edward de Weaver於1380年出生在英國英格蘭爾奧維時，他的父親Thomas de Weaver20歲，母親Isabella de Fulleshurst20歲。他於1400年2月3日與Elizabeth de Booth在英國英格蘭越可爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1435年在英國英格蘭爾米德列維其享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Edward de Weaver于1380年出生在英国英格兰爾奧維时，他的父亲Thomas de Weaver20岁，母亲Isabella de Fulleshurst20岁。他于1400年2月3日与Elizabeth de Booth在英国英格兰越可爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1435年在英国英格兰爾米德列維其享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Weaver
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name, from an agent derivative of Middle English weven ‘to weave’ (Old English wefan).

English: habitational name from a place on the Weaver river in Cheshire, now called Weaver Hall but recorded simply as Weuere in the 13th and 14th centuries. The river name is from Old English wēfer(e) ‘winding stream’.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘weaver’, for example German Weber , Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic) Tkacz or Tkach , Hungarian Takács (see Takacs ), and Slovenian Tkalec, Tekavec or Veber .

variants.name: Weber
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a weaver, Middle High German wëber, German Weber, an agent derivative of weben ‘to weave’. This form of the surname is also established in many other parts of Europe, notably in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, and the Netherlands. In Hungary it is mostly spelled Wéber and Véber. In Russia, Slovenia, Czechia, Slovakia, and Croatia it is (also) established in the Slavicized form Veber , which is in Czechia also spelled Vebr. Compare Waber , Weaber , Weaver , Webber , and Webre .

History: As a name of ultimately Swiss German origin the surname Weber is also established among the American Mennonites. However, descendants of the early Mennonite settlers, who came to PA in the early 18th century, mostly bear the Americanized form of the name (see Weaver ).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edward de Weaver was born in 1380, in Over, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas de Weaver, was 20 and his mother, Isabella de Fulleshurst, was 20. He married Elizabeth de Booth on 3 February 1400, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1435, in Middlewich, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edward de Weaver est né en 1380, à Over, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas de Weaver, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Isabella de Fulleshurst, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de Booth le 3 février 1400, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1435, à Middlewich, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Edward de Weaver 1380 in Over, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de Weaver 20 und seine Mutter Isabella de Fulleshurst 20. Er hat am 3. Februar 1400 in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth de Booth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1435 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Middlewich, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edward de Weaver è nato il nel 1380 nel Over, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Thomas de Weaver, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Isabella de Fulleshurst, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth de Booth il 3 febbraio 1400, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1435, nel Middlewich, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 55 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Edward de Weaverが1380年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアオバーで生まれた時，父Thomas de Weaverは20歳，母Isabella de Fulleshurstは20歳であった。1400年2月3日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアでElizabeth de Boothと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1435年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアミッドルイチで55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edward de Weaver(이)가 1380년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Weaver(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella de Fulleshurst(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1400년 2월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 Elizabeth de Booth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1435년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 미들루이치에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edward de Weaver nasceu em 1380, em Over, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas de Weaver, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Isabella de Fulleshurst, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth de Booth em 3 de fevereiro de 1400, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1435, em Middlewich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edward de Weaver родился в 1380 г. в местности овир, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas de Weaver было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella de Fulleshurst, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Booth 3 февраля 1400 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1435 г. в местности миддльвич, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 55 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Edward de Weaver nació en 1380, en Over, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas de Weaver, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Isabella de Fulleshurst, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Elizabeth de Booth el 3 de febrero de 1400, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1435, en Middlewich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 55 años.
